user_query:
Please read the print failure notes in input/print_failures.jsonl and do a full diagnostic and pattern analysis. For each case, produce:
1) A structured diagnosis array in output/diagnoses.json where each item preserves the input id and includes: printer, material, description, a ranked_diagnoses list of at least 3 hypothesized failure types with confidence scores (0–1), top_causes (2–4 concise bullets), and slicer-specific fixes for all three slicers (keys: "PrusaSlicer", "Cura", "OrcaSlicer"). Tailor the fixes to the top-ranked failure and include concrete parameter suggestions (e.g., retraction distance/speed, temperature, travel speed, brim width, fan %, first-layer settings, speed/acceleration/jerk). Keep parameter names clear and use numeric values where appropriate.
2) A personal failure log at output/failure-log.json with a top-level "failures" array. Include one entry per case with: id, timestamp (ISO 8601), printer, material, failure_type (your top pick), description, slicer_settings (your proposed adjustments for the user’s preferred slicer), fixed_by (a short plan summarizing your changes), and notes (including any optional hardware checks, but prefer slicer changes first).
3) A markdown report at output/report.md summarizing: counts by failure type; counts by printer/material combination; top recurring combinations; and 3–5 actionable recommendations. Include a short section explaining how you mapped the reported symptoms to likely failure modes.

Important:
- Do not remove any cases; keep ids consistent with input.
- Provide slicer-specific settings for PrusaSlicer, Cura, and OrcaSlicer for every case (even if the user’s preferred slicer is listed).
- Focus on slicer and process changes first; only mention hardware checks as optional notes.
- Use relative paths only and write the three deliverables exactly to output/diagnoses.json, output/failure-log.json, and output/report.md.
